raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this formal complaint to request regulatory review of Barclays Bank Delaware for its failure to conduct a thorough and reasonable investigation of disputed credit card transactions and its failure to follow proper dispute-resolution procedures as required under applicable consumer protection laws. 
On or about XX/XX/year>, my checked luggage was lost by an airline during international travel. That luggage contained my wallet, multiple credit cards, and one identification card. I did not have possession of these cards during the period in which unauthorized or unrecognized transactions subsequently occurred. 
After discovering unrecognized charges on my Aviator Mastercard, I promptly notified the issuer and formally disputed all transactions that posted after XX/XX/year>. I made clear that the charges occurred after my credit cards were lost. 
During the dispute process, I explicitly offered to provide supporting documentation from the airline confirming the loss of my luggage and its contents, which directly substantiates my claim that I did not have control or possession of the credit cards when the disputed charges occurred. 
However, Barclays declined to review or accept this evidence, stating that it was not needed, and proceeded to deny the dispute and close the case.
